food_subgroup: Cream cracker biscuit — wheat flour, palm oil shortening, baked, packaged (NOVA Class 3)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn URI exists for cream crackers as a distinct product class. Closest parent would be a generic biscuit/cracker node; URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cream cracker biscuit (closest parent match in FoodEx2 hierarchy)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0HAD is the closest defensible FoodEx2 code for cream crackers; no exact product-level code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cream cracker biscuit product; null assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 Khong Guan Cream Crackers weighing approximately 30g, providing around 138 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Khong Guan Cream Crackers — Singapore Product Nutrition Label; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Crackers, cream (Item 18963)
nutrient_source.organization: Khong Guan Biscuit Factory (Singapore) Ltd; United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from Khong Guan Singapore product nutrition label (2024 print). Micronutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cream crackers (Item 18963) to confirm plausibility. Label values take precedence where they differ.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for cream crackers (refined wheat, baked)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 65 (medium) is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Am J Clin Nutr) for plain cream crackers made from refined white wheat flour, baked, low fibre. Available carbohydrate per 30g serving ≈ 19.2g; GL = (65 × 19.2) / 100 ≈ 12.5, rounded to 12.0. No Khong Guan-specific GI test exists; closest analogous food (plain cream cracker, refined wheat) used from approved GI source.

hypertension.guidance: At a standard 4-cracker (30g) serving, Khong Guan Cream Crackers contribute approximately 200mg sodium — one of the lower sodium loads among packaged snacks in Singapore — making them cautiously acceptable for hypertensive patients when portion is limited to 4–6 crackers and not paired with high-sodium spreads.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic index ~65 (medium), glycemic load ~12 per 30g serving, low dietary fibre (0.5g per 30g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of ~65 and GL of ~12 per 4-cracker serving, cream crackers produce a moderate postprandial glucose response; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to 4 crackers per sitting, pair with a protein or healthy-fat spread to blunt the glycaemic rise, and avoid repeated servings given the negligible fibre content (0.5g per 30g) that provides little intrinsic glucose buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.0g per 100g (2.4g per 30g serving) from palm oil; trans fat confirmed zero
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 30g serving delivers approximately 2.4g saturated fat from palm oil shortening, which is modest at a single serving but accumulates with larger portions; patients with cardiovascular disease should restrict to 4 crackers per occasion, note the absence of trans fat as a positive attribute, and avoid high-saturated-fat toppings such as butter or full-fat cheese.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Khong Guan Cream Crackers are a plain, crispy, flaky wheat-flour biscuit produced by Khong Guan Biscuit Factory (Singapore) Ltd, a heritage brand established in 1947 with near-universal household penetration across all Singaporean demographics. The cracker is baked from refined wheat flour, palm oil shortening, salt, and leavening agents, yielding a dry, light, mildly savoury texture with no sweetness. It is sold in the iconic round tin and in foil-wrapped packets. Per 100g: 460 kcal, 8.0g protein, 16.7g fat (8.0g saturated from palm oil, 0g trans fat), 64.0g carbohydrate, 0.7g sugar, 1.7g dietary fibre, 667mg sodium. A standard serving of 4 crackers (~30g) provides approximately 138 kcal, 2.4g protein, 5.0g fat, 19.2g carbohydrate, and ~200mg sodium. Glycemic index ~65 (medium), glycemic load ~12 per serving. Clinically: sodium load is low relative to most packaged snacks, making it cautiously acceptable for hypertensive patients at 4–6 cracker portions. For Type 2 Diabetes, the moderate GI and low fibre require portion control and pairing with protein or fat spreads. Saturated fat from palm oil is modest per serving but accumulates. The cracker is vegan, halal, and dairy-free. Culturally, it is the quintessential Singapore 'illness food' served with Milo or Horlicks, a CNY tin staple, and a common afternoon tea snack across all ethnic groups. NOVA Class 3 (processed food). Source: Khong Guan Singapore product label (2024); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cream crackers, Item 18963) for cross-reference.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Khong Guan Singapore product nutrition label (2024), an exact match for this packaged product. Micronutrients cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for cream crackers. GI value drawn from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for the closest analogous food (plain cream cracker, refined wheat). All values are internally consistent and plausible.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Khong Guan Singapore product label and is not available in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cream crackers as of 2024. 2. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content is not declared on the product label and is not available in the cross-referenced USDA SR Legacy entry for this specific product. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per Khong Guan Singapore product label (2024). 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per product label; cream crackers use palm oil shortening with no animal-derived fat. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0: confirmed not present per product label and consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for plain cream crackers. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain cream crackers (refined white wheat, baked); no Khong Guan-specific GI clinical trial has been published. 7. Portion note: the input record lists two serving sizes (25g and 30g); this record uses 30g (~4 standard crackers) as the canonical serving, consistent with the llm_context natural language description and the glycemic load calculation. The 25g figure in the input portions array may reflect a slightly smaller cracker size variant.
